source.title: Insensitivity of Place Cells to the Value of Spatial Goals in a Two-Choice Flexible Navigation Task

confidence_rationale: Carefully controlled negative result: rats prefer the higher-value goal but CA1/CA3 place cells do not encode goal value. Constrains the generalization — value-modulation of the place ensemble is not a universal property, so the hippocampal map cannot be assumed to tag arbitrary affect dimensions onto generic place cells.
failure_signatures: Hippocampal place cells in dorsal CA1/CA3 do not encode goal value, even when the animal's behaviour clearly tracks value.
If REE were to assume the place ensemble itself carries a graded valence/value signal, this falsifies that model: value-tagging happens elsewhere and is read out by other circuits.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Dorsal CA1 and CA3 place cells form a value-free spatial map: they do not modulate firing rate with goal value despite the animal preferring higher-value goals.
mapping.ree_translation: The hippocampal map encodes space independently of the value associated with each location. Affect-stream channels (SD-011, SD-012) cannot simply be 'colour' overlays on the place ensemble — they must occupy distinct populations or read-out circuits, which is consistent with the dedicated-population pattern Gauthier & Tank found for reward identity.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Goal-value signals could exist in other hippocampal subregions (ventral CA1) or in non-place cells not analysed here. The negative result speaks to the generic-modulation hypothesis, not to whether dedicated subpopulations exist.
mapping.source_context: Long-Evans rats, two-choice continuous place-navigation task with goals of different reward magnitude, single-unit recording in dorsal CA1 and CA3.

confidence_components.notes: Direct test of the generic-value-modulation hypothesis with a clear behavioural marker (preference) confirming the rat tracks value. The negative result is informative — it forces the SD-011-generalization hypothesis to take the dedicated-population shape rather than the place-cell-tagging shape.
